manifest.name: Persistent Graph

name: Persistent Graph

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Version 0.3.2 of Persistent Graph fixes some long-standing issues and adds some requested features.  All this work was gracefully provided by @Gyrandola.

## New features
- Graph nodes can now be pinned using the right click menu.
- Save/restore commands are now available as buttons in the graph view.
- There is a new settings option to automatically save graph positions.
- Graph simulation commands can now be disabled in settings.

## Bugs fixed
- Fixes graph structure being lost after renaming a folder
- Fixed graph positions not automatically restored if graph isn't in the leftmost pane when starting app
- Fixed "save graph node position" command breaking the save when not used in graph view.

Find more details in the pull request: https://github.com/Sanqui/obsidian-persistent-graph/pull/30

Report issues: https://github.com/Sanqui/obsidian-persistent-graph/issues
releases.name: 0.3.2 - Revival update (thanks Gyrandola!)

releases.author: Sanqui
releases.description:
v0.2.0 of Persistent Graph featuring contributions from @zjhcn:
- It is now possible to save the graph settings (filters, forces etc.) together with the positions of nodes.
- If you're using the Workspaces plugin, you can now save your graph configuration per-workspace.

Find both features in the settings for this plugin.
releases.name: v0.2.0 - Workspaces support (thanks zjhcn!)

releases.author: Sanqui
releases.description: No changes besides a better way to register event.
